message:
[high] slack: pin base_url origin and reject empty tokens in configure

handle_configure accepted any base_url override with no validation,
so a misconfigured or attacker-controlled configure payload could
redirect Slack API traffic to an arbitrary host. It also accepted
present-but-empty token/app_token values, producing silent Bearer ""
Authorization headers downstream.

- New validate_slack_base_url() pins the override host to slack.com
  (localhost/127.0.0.1/::1 still accepted for test harnesses) and
  requires https on any non-local host. Empty overrides are rejected.
- token and app_token are now trimmed and empty values are rejected
  at configure time instead of silently proceeding.
- Five new unit tests cover accept (slack.com + localhost), reject
  (foreign host, http on public host, substring smuggle, empty).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -36,6 +36,7 @@ thiserror.workspace = true
 tracing.workspace = true
 tracing-subscriber.workspace = true
 percent-encoding.workspace = true
+url.workspace = true
 uuid.workspace = true
 
 [dev-dependencies]
